acceptance_criteria: A mutation is durably fsynced to the WAL before the caller's Append call returns, and before the corresponding in-memory/on-disk state change is considered committed.

rows.requirement: Catalog Put mutation record type: encode/decode round trip (FileID + opaque encoded CatalogRecord bytes)
Catalog Delete mutation record type: encode/decode round trip (FileID only)
BTree Insert mutation record type: encode/decode round trip (Path + FileID)
BTree Delete mutation record type: encode/decode round trip (Path only)
Edge case: empty string / empty blob fields encode/decode correctly
fsync-before-apply: apply callback only fires after the WAL record is durably observable on disk (proven via independent ReadSegment call inside the callback, not by code inspection)
apply() error after a successful durable append: error propagated, offset still returned, and the WAL record remains durably present on disk (recovery-replay-friendly semantics)
Writer.Append failure (e.g. oversized record) must skip apply entirely — apply never runs if nothing was durably logged
No regression to 1.3.1's segment writer / rotation / oversized-record / resume-numbering behavior
rows.test: TestSegmentWriter, TestAppendOversizedRecordHardErrors, TestOpenWriterResumesExistingSegments (all in writer_test.go, unmodified)
